seed_problem: Assign frequencies to transmitters minimizing total interference conflicts under a channel budget; checker scores baseline_conflicts/achieved.
mutation: composite: keep the frequency-assignment goal but (a) generalize the conflict model from pure co-channel graph coloring to a weighted span-2 model with both co-channel penalty p and adjacent-channel penalty q per pair, and (b) generate the interference graph from a random geometric arena layout with a few heavy-tailed 'hero rig' links, so a small channel budget C makes zero-interference impossible and greedy / min-conflicts / multi-restart heuristics diverge.
title: Arena Airwaves: Channel Assignment for the Grand Final
strategies: do-nothing baseline: assign every rig channel 1 (worst-case co-channel interference == the scoring baseline B)
degree/weight-ordered greedy: process rigs by descending interference weight and pick the channel minimizing added penalty against already-assigned neighbours (one pass)
min-conflicts local search: greedy init, then repeatedly move each rig to its least-penalty channel until no sweep improves the total interference
randomized multi-restart: run min-conflicts from several seeded random initial assignments and keep the lowest-interference assignment found
